human_verification.notes: Verified aviation analytics boundary handling, transportation research source-class coverage, metric-definition and reconciliation controls, unsupported-claim refusal, movement and chain-of-custody handling, safety and clearance escalation, and scenario-to-ability mapping completeness.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit metric-definition, report-assembly, source-reconciliation, unsupported-claim, movement custody, and safety or clearance escalation behavior.
Required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and transportation-and-logistics abilities are fully covered and mapped.

specialist_owned_evidence: Metric-definition logic is explicit and tied to named aviation, airport, cargo, customs, safety, and service-quality source records.
Conflicting, stale, incomplete, or denominator-ambiguous data is escalated instead of normalized into unsupported reporting.
Cargo movement and handoff analytics preserve chain-of-custody and documentation constraints without implying clearance authority.
Dispatch, customs, cargo security, maintenance, safety, customer operations, legal, and disclosure boundaries route to adjacent owners with structured evidence.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-U.S. jurisdiction scope and tenant-specific metric definitions are captured as runtime context requirements rather than guessed in-pack.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with explicit promotion criteria for a future retained-implementation package.
